title: Buildx Builders, Multi-Platform Images, Bake, and OCI Outputs · Container Boundary Trace: Docker Qemu Native Crosscompile
summary: A CLI release must support linux/amd64 and linux/arm64 without silently shipping an emulated, untested, or single-platform image. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker qemu native crosscompile increment in multi-platform build matrix and manifest evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose emulation, multiple native nodes, or language cross-compilation and verify build and target platform separately.
Relate OS, architecture, variant, image manifest, index, local store, registry, pull selection, and runtime compatibility.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Docker Qemu Native Crosscompile · Theory Docker Qemu Native Crosscompile
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory release must publish linux/amd64 and linux/arm64. The builder uses emulation, local load, undeclared platform inputs, and no per-platform tests. Define Bake targets, exporters, attestations, and index verification. Competency probe: Choose emulation, multiple native nodes, or language cross-compilation and verify build and target platform separately.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example release must publish linux/amd64 and linux/arm64. The builder uses emulation, local load, undeclared platform inputs, and no per-platform tests. Define Bake targets, exporters, attestations, and index verification. Competency probe: Choose emulation, multiple native nodes, or language cross-compilation and verify build and target platform separately. A defensible solution applies Docker Qemu Native Crosscompile, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to multi-platform build matrix and manifest evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-worked-docker-multiplatform-buildx-bake-2
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-multiplatform-buildx-bake-2
    artifact: docker-bake.hcl
    decision: worked docker multiplatform buildx bake 2
    scenario-variant: 1
    environment: Docker Desktop VM with synchronized source files
    fault-injection: send termination during an in-flight durable operation
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked2
    change: |
      target "release" {
      platforms = ["linux/amd64", "linux/arm64"]
      attest = ["type=provenance", "type=sbom"]
      output = ["type=registry"]
      }
    verify: docker buildx bake --print release && docker buildx imagetools inspect candidate
    failure: Reject architecture emulation as native test evidence, local image load for a multi-platform index, or platform-dependent inputs without declarations.
    repair: Inspect builder capabilities, make target platform explicit, test each artifact, publish the index, and verify per-platform digest selection.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A QEMU build result proves native runtime performance and compatibility. This fails the Docker Qemu Native Crosscompile contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
